briefing.opening: Good morning, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for April 26, 2026.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Climate Change Week Operations and the Promotion of Carbon Neutral Lifestyle Practices
sections.content: Various local governments across the country are showing active engagement in responding to climate change. Cities such as Yeosu and Yeongdo District have operated 'UN Climate Change Convention Climate Week' and 'Climate Change Week,' initiating global climate response discussions and launching campaigns like the '10-minute break for Earth' [1, 3, 5, 9, 11]. Cheongdo-eup and Gwangmyeong City have promoted carbon neutrality awareness and encouraged citizen participation through lifestyle practice campaigns such as 'Carbon Jogging' and 'Climate Volunteer Plalking Day' [12, 19, 28, 31, 32]. These community-based efforts are becoming crucial stepping stones for the transition to a carbon-neutral society.

sections.title: ⚠️ Accelerating Impacts of Climate Change and Domestic and International Adaptation Efforts
sections.content: The impacts of climate change are becoming increasingly visible both domestically and internationally, necessitating urgent adaptation efforts. Domestically, with frequent localized torrential rains projected for this summer, inspections of rainwater drainage systems are underway [16]. Sinan County is taking measures to change the species of fish farmed in offshore cages in response to climate change [2, 8]. Chuncheon City has also initiated a service to establish agricultural policy directions to respond to the major climate change transition, signaling a full-scale paradigm shift in the agricultural sector [4, 13, 18]. Internationally, aquaculture farms in Vietnam's Ca Mau region are facing mass mortality of shrimp and crabs due to heatwaves. In the Democratic Republic of Congo, climate change is hindering beekeeping, and the possibility of new snake species appearing in Belarus has been raised, indicating escalating threats across entire ecosystems [17, 47, 50]. Such widespread impacts of climate change underscore the necessity for comprehensive adaptation strategies.

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and a New Energy Paradigm
sections.content: Instability in international affairs, such as the Middle East conflict, and the explosive increase in electricity demand from Artificial Intelligence (AI) are acting as major drivers accelerating the global transition to renewable energy [35, 39, 40]. Domestically, Gyeongbuk's high renewable energy potential is gaining attention, and concrete transition efforts have begun, including the 100% renewable energy conversion of Jeju Lava Seawater Complex and the promotion of renewable energy-based AI data centers [33, 37, 44]. Furthermore, on the 40th anniversary of the Chernobyl nuclear accident, calls for halting new nuclear power plant construction and expanding renewable energy continue, while a 'Renewable Energy Special Act Enactment Forum' also raised the need to reduce administrative costs due to solar power regulations [36, 41]. Meanwhile, support for labor transition aligned with the carbon-neutral and AI era is gaining momentum, and 'Auto China 2026' showcased a feast of electric vehicle technologies, indicating that efforts to optimize electricity use and transition to clean energy are being pursued from various angles [20, 45]. However, finding a balance between surging electricity demand and achieving net-zero targets remains a significant challenge, as exemplified by the conflict between UK government departments over AI data center energy demand [55].

sections.title: 🌍 Discussions on International Climate Cooperation and Scientific Insights
sections.content: The international community is accelerating cooperation and scientific inquiry to address climate change. A climate change conference was held in Colombia, and Thailand led sustainable travel discussions at GSTC2026, gathering global tourism leaders to discuss climate change and destination management [46, 51]. Meanwhile, USask researchers are gaining insights into climate change through unexplored trace fossils, and concerns have been raised about the possibility of global warming leading to the 'collapse' of the Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation (AMOC) [49, 54]. The collaborative tracking of migratory birds by Indigenous peoples in Australia and Alaska demonstrates international solidarity for biodiversity conservation, emphasizing the importance of global climate response efforts alongside the role of Korea's Presidential Committee on Climate Change Response [6, 24, 30, 56].

sections.title: 🌱 Education and Technological Innovation for a Carbon-Neutral Society
sections.content: Efforts to transition to a carbon-neutral society are broadly underway through education and technological innovation. Gyeongju Carbon Neutrality Support Center is expanding environmental education by presenting 'Visiting Carbon Neutrality ON Plays' tailored to children's perspectives [15, 21, 26]. Daegu City is recruiting its '3rd Youth Supporters' to lead carbon-neutral practices, encouraging citizen participation [22, 27]. The Korea Limestone New Materials Research Institute is actively engaged in R&D for technological advancement, including embarking on the development of composite materials that will be key to carbon neutrality [29]. These educational and technological innovations are spreading carbon neutrality awareness [23] and laying a crucial foundation for the sustainability of future society [25].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing once again reminds us of the importance of adaptation efforts and the search for innovative solutions, which are actively underway both domestically and internationally amidst the complex threats of climate change. This sustained attention and action will pave the way for a sustainable future. This concludes the climate briefing for April 26, 2026.

articles.original_title: [오토 차이나 2026]“봐도 봐도 끝이 없다”…축구장 50개 크기 공간서 펼쳐진 전기차 기술의 향연
articles.description: The organizing committee announced the exhibition venue has doubled in size compared to two years ago. A total of 1,451 vehicles, including 181 world premiere models and 71 concept cars, were showcased. Global automakers were present, with Hyundai Motor participating from Korea and unveiling the 'IONIQ V.' The 'Auto China 2026' auto show, which opened on April 24th in Beijing, China, highlighted the dominance of electric vehicles.

summary.phenomenon: The 'Auto China 2026' auto show opened on April 24th at the China International Exhibition Center in Beijing, showcasing a total of 1,451 electric vehicles, including 181 world premiere models and 71 concept cars, across an exhibition space twice the size of the previous event, equivalent to 50 football fields. Hyundai Motor participated from Korea, unveiling its 'IONIQ V' model.

articles.original_title: ‘The birds are a global citizen’: Indigenous groups in Australia and Alaska team up to track a feathered adventurer’s epic journey
articles.description: A new knowledge-sharing project aims to ensure the survival of the migratory short-tailed shearwaterShort-tailed shearwaters used to blacken the skies on the south-west coast of Australia, so abundant were they in their coastal homes each Djilba season – the time in the calendar of the Noongar peoples between August and September, when days shift from blustery cold and wet winds to warmer weather.In Wudjari Noongar, the language of the traditional owners of this place, they call Kepa Kurl, but w

summary.phenomenon: Indigenous peoples from Australia and Alaska are collaborating on a new knowledge-sharing project to ensure the survival of the migratory short-tailed shearwater. In the past, during the Djilba season (August-September) in the Noongar calendar, short-tailed shearwaters were so abundant on Australia's southwest coast that they would blacken the skies.
summary.outlook: The new knowledge-sharing project aims to ensure the survival of the migratory short-tailed shearwater.
